steps.title: Identify the central claim about economic knowledge
steps.content: The passage argues that economic knowledge is central to democracy and public functioning—so important that it “cannot remain the domain only of experts.” This core claim signals what the blank word must do: explain why this knowledge is unsuitable for expert monopoly.

steps.title: Select the word that justifies this claim
steps.content:
Knowledge of economics is NOT superfluous—it's essential, not extra or unnecessary—which is exactly why it cannot be left only to experts; the public must have this knowledge.

The other choices either name irrelevant properties (equitable = fairness, commonplace = frequency) or point to the wrong factor (unattainable = whether something is achievable, not whether it's essential).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
